title: Urban congestion relief experiments through routing-app interventions
data_sources: Google Maps aggregated and anonymized traffic data (proprietary)
UK DESNZ Greenhouse Gas Conversion Factors 2023

data_scope.segments_per_city: ~100 targeted; affected set covers ~80% of city traffic
data_scope.data_availability: primary traffic data confidential; source data for Fig. 2 provided as XLSX; analysis code open on GitHub

core_conclusion: A switchback experiment across ~100 congested road segments in 10 major US cities shows that rerouting less than 2% of Google Maps trips produces a city-average 2% speed increase on targeted segments, 0.7% drop in total travel times, and potential annual CO2-equivalent savings exceeding 1,000 tonnes per city in most locations. Marginal, network-aware routing interventions measurably improve road-network efficiency at city scale without meaningfully harming rerouted drivers.
why_it_matters: First large-scale empirical proof of network-aware routing benefits; congestion relief via software without infrastructure investment; quantified environmental dividend from speed gains; rigorous switchback causal design establishing a reproducible smart-city experiment template; demonstrates traffic dispersion (not just displacement) onto underutilized roads.
why_nature: Multi-city (10 cities, 6 months) empirical evidence for a generalizable urban-systems claim; bridges mobility efficiency and sustainability goals; immediately actionable findings for city planners and navigation platforms; demonstrates the switchback paradigm as a reproducible template for empirical smart-city research; open analysis code.
added_value_to_my_research: RL-on-graph routing policy with Wardrop physics constraint -> transferable GeoAI routing operator for data-sparse cities
Spatial equity audit of volume-change maps (MGWR/spatial entropy) -> who bears the burden of rerouted traffic by income/race
Fuse near-real-time emission monitoring as live reward signal for routing -> closed-loop urban health intervention pipeline
Mode-shift extension using GTFS accessibility (2SFCA) -> feasibility map for transit-or-car rerouting by neighborhood
Calibrate induced-demand digital twin to paper's causal estimates -> long-run EV/healthcare facility siting analog

related.relation: complementary empirical work on data-driven traffic control and carbon reduction at city scale

related.relation: uses mobility data to quantify urban accessibility amid congestion; equity framing connects to routing-intervention equity analysis

figures.caption: Routing intervention workflow: costing phase adds externality penalties to targeted segments, rerouting a small fraction of trips to comparable alternatives with similar travel times.
Posterior distributions of treatment effects on segment speeds (a), fuel consumption rates (b), and total trip travel times (c) across all cities and hours.
Traffic dispersion in Atlanta: original vs. alternate route paths and net volume changes on segments; flows move from central high-volume roads to dispersed peripheral segments.
